mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates directly increase the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, compressing forward S&P 500 valuations and producing negative returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >55% of months following a 25bps rate hike, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative equity repricing within one week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in a single month
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized domestic demand growth that drives corporate revenue expectations higher, generating positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects capacity constraints overriding demand, causing equity markets to price in stagflation risks and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns do not materialize when INDPRO declines during periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.5%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising corporate earnings capacity, which consistently supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following positive PAYEMS revisions, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all market conditions.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index reading above 195.0 indicates persistent inflation that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 195.0, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling real personal consumption expenditures signals corporate profit margin compression from inventory buildup, triggering negative equity repricing over the next month.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence fails to predict negative 20-day returns in >50% of quarters, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates rapid capacity utilization that boosts corporate earnings forecasts, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns do not occur in >55% of weeks following INDPRO acceleration during recovery phases, or the signal reverses direction in subsequent quarters.
